8821311110.translation: "A picturesque landscape that seemed to surpass itself in richness and beauty with every curve of the river — the most cheerful sky that created a May day in the middle of February — charming gardens and tasteful country houses without number, which adorned both banks of the Brenta — behind us majestic Venice, with a hundred towers and masts springing from the water, all of this gave us the most magnificent spectacle in the world."
8821311110.text_linked: „Eine mahleriſche [[Landſchaft|nouns/Landschaft]], die mit jeder Kruͤmmung des [[Fluſſes|nouns/Fluss]] ſich an [[Reichthum|nouns/Reichtum]] und [[Schoͤnheit|nouns/Schönheit]] zu [[übertreffen|verbs/übertreffen#2]] ſchien — der [[heiterſte|adjectives/heiter#1]] [[Himmel|nouns/Himmel#1]], der mitten im Hornung einen Maientag bildete — [[reizende|adjectives/reizend]] [[Gaͤrten|nouns/Garten]] und geſchmackvolle [[Landhaͤuſer|nouns/Landhaus]] ohne [[Zahl|nouns/Zahl#1]], welche beyde [[Ufer|nouns/Ufer]] der Brenta ſchmuͤcken — hinter uns das majeſtaͤtiſche Venedig, mit [[hundert|numerals/hundert#1]] aus dem [[Waſſer|nouns/Wasser]] ſpringenden [[Thuͤrmen|nouns/Turm]] und [[Maſten|nouns/Mast_füttern]], alles dieß gab uns das [[herrlichſte|adjectives/herrlich#1]] [[Schauſpiel|nouns/Schauspiel]] von der [[Welt|nouns/Welt#3]].“

88db0d660e.translation: "Although the realm of nature and the realm of purposes were also thought to be united under a supreme head, and thereby the latter would no longer remain a mere idea but would acquire true reality, this would indeed bring to the former the gain of a strong incentive, but never an increase in its inner worth; for despite this, even this sole absolute lawgiver would always have to be conceived as judging the worth of rational beings only according to their disinterested conduct, prescribed to them solely from that idea itself."
